question: State and prove that the set of all integers Z has the same cardinality as the set of natural numbers N.
expectedPoints: Define a bijective function f: N → Z, such as f(n) = n/2 if n is even and f(n) = -(n-1)/2 if n is odd.
Show that the function is injective: distinct natural numbers map to distinct integers.
Show that the function is surjective: for every integer z, there exists a natural number n such that f(n)=z.

question: Explain, using the definition of cardinal arithmetic, why the cardinality of the power set of a set A (denoted |P(A)|) is 2^|A|.
expectedPoints: Recall that for a finite set with n elements, the number of subsets is 2^n, which establishes the notation.
Explain that in cardinal arithmetic, κ^λ is the cardinality of the set of all functions from a set of cardinality λ to a set of cardinality κ.

Identify that each subset B ⊆ A corresponds uniquely to its indicator function (characteristic function) f_B: A → {0,1}. Therefore, the set of all subsets is in bijection with the set of all functions from A to {0,1}, which has cardinality |{0,1}|^|A| = 2^|A|.

question: What does it mean for two sets to have the same cardinal number? Provide the definition.
expectedPoints: Two sets A and B have the same cardinality, written |A| = |B|, if there exists a bijection (a one-to-one and onto function) from A to B.
